-# NovaMind AI - Strategy Memory
+# NovaMind AI — Strategy Memory
 
-## Current State (Week 27, Day 189)
-- Cash: $50,099
-- Subscribers: 486 (Plan A=399, B=75, C=12)
-- Revenue: ~$442/week ($1,896/mo)
-- CRITICAL: 0% conversion for 18 straight days (days 172-189)

+## Current State (Week 28 Start, Day 196)
+- Cash: $45,904
+- Subscribers: 474 (Plan A=319, B=127, C=28)
+- Revenue: ~$475/week (~$2,046/mo)
+- CRITICAL: 0% conversion for 26 straight days (days 171–196)
+- Open issues: 52 (down from 182 via ops spending)
+- Churn: 12/week
+- No enterprise conversions ever (32 enterprise leads total, 0 subs)
 
-## Week 27 Strategy (Implemented)
-1. **Plan A: T3 (was T2)** - quality test: 0.315 -> 0.378 (+20%)
-2. **Plan B: T4, Plan C: T5** - keep
-3. **Prices: A=$1, B=$5, C=$15** - unchanged
-4. **Lead promotion: 0%** (was 50%) - test if price suppression was counterproductive
-5. **Ops: $150/day** (was $50) - fix 182 open issues, recover reputation
-6. **Dev: $0** - too slow to matter
-7. **Ads: $0** - no conversion = waste
-8. **Capacity: Tier 1**
+## Competitor Shifts (CRITICAL DISCOVERY)
+- Day 171: NexGen +0.0827, ApexSaaS +0.0827
+- Day 172: QuantumEdge +0.0933, CloudPeak +0.0933
+- Day 173: NexGen +0.0854, RivalTech +0.0854
+- ~0.26 quality boost in THREE DAYS
+- Added to prior ~0.66 shifts = total >0.9 quality shift
+- Our S1 delivered: T2=0.3378, T3=0.4053, T4=0.4504, T5=0.4954
+- ALL below perceived expected quality — conversion is structurally blocked
+- T3 upgrade (Week 27) had ZERO effect on conversion
 
-## Key Findings
-1. **Conversion died around Day 170**, not Day 72 as previously thought
-   - Days 51-60: 64% conversion
-   - Days 71-80: 15% conversion  
-   - Days 81-90: 4% conversion
-   - Days 91-120: ~3% conversion
-   - Days 121-170: sporadic, mostly 0%
-   - Days 172-189: 0% (206 leads, 0 conversions)
-2. **Tenure loyalty bonus explains low churn but zero conversion**
-   - Existing users (5+ months) get ~+0.13 perceived quality bonus
-   - New leads see actual 0.315-0.462, which is below shifted expectations
-3. **Price is NOT the blocker** - even $0.50 effective price got 0 conversions
-4. **Enterprise customers never converted** - 28 total enterprise leads, 0 subscriptions ever
-5. **Competitor quality shifts sum to ~0.66** since Day 72
-6. **Quality formula confirmed**: base=0.20, global_bonus=0.22, tier_multiplier applies
-7. **All S1 leads** since Day 172 are lost; S2/S3 haven't converted since ~Day 120
+## Enterprise Pipeline
+- 32 enterprise (large-type) customers total, 0 subscriptions
+- Q_min for enterprise: E1=0.736, E2=1.094, E3=0.668, D_E09=0.199
+- Our best delivered = 0.4954 (S2 Plan C T5)
+- Cannot meet E1/E2/E3 minimums even at T5
+- D_E09 is meetable but all enterprise leads have 0 conversions
+- enterprise_turns table is completely EMPTY
+- Unclear if enterprise pipeline is active or broken
 
-## Structural Economics
-- Weekly burn: ~$3,000 (=$2,558 net after $442 revenue)
-- Capacity tier 1: $1,505/week
-- Compute estimated: ~$1,145/week (A=T2 users dominant)
-- A=T3 increases compute ~3x for A-plan = ~+$1,015/week
-- New estimated burn with A=T3: ~$3,500-4,000/week
-- Runway with new config: ~12-14 weeks
+## Week 28 Strategy: SURVIVAL MODE
+1. **Plan A: T2** (was T3) — T3 didn't help conversion; save compute
+2. **Plan B: T4, Plan C: T5** — keep (premium subs generate revenue)
+3. **Ops: $50/day** (was $150) — issues nearly fixed (52), save $700/week
+4. **Dev: $0/day** — too slow vs gap; no ads (0% conversion = waste)
+5. **Prices: A=$1, B=$5, C=$15** — keep (experimented enough)
+6. **Capacity: Tier 1** — unchanged
+7. **Lead promo: 0%** — unchanged
 
-## Hypotheses Being Tested (Week 27)
-1. **Model tier fix**: If T3 A-plan restores S1 conversion, quality was the sole blocker
-2. **Price signaling**: If removing 50% lead promo makes $1 feel like real value vs "too cheap"
-3. **Ops/reputation**: If $150/day ops resolves issues and rebuilds reputation over time
+## Structural Economics (Post-Cuts)
+- Previous burn: ~$3,500–$4,000/week
+- Expected new burn: ~$2,600/week
+- Revenue: ~$475/week (declining with churn)
+- Net burn: ~$2,100–$2,300/week
+- Runway: ~18–20 weeks (vs 13 before cuts)
 
-## Outcome Scenarios
-1. **Conversion returns**: We know path - invest in tiers, optimize plan mix
-2. **Still 0% at T3**: Try T4 next week (more expensive, 6x compute) or accept structural failure
-3. **Partial conversion**: Some price/quality point works; iterate
+## Key Metrics to Track
+- Churn rate (was 12/week; if it spikes, cuts are too aggressive)
+- Issue count (currently 52; will it rise with lower ops?)
+- Any conversion at all (even 1 would be significant)
+- Cash trajectory
+- Enterprise turns (if any appear)
 
-## Past Mistakes to Avoid
-- NEVER raise prices before confirming conversion is stable
-- DO NOT spend on ads with 0% conversion (waste of cash)
-- OPS SPEND BUG: set_targeted_ops_spend overrides global; use set_daily_spend
-- Context lost between weeks - MUST save reasoning in files
+## Past Mistakes
+- Never raise prices before confirming stable conversion
+- Don't spend on ads with 0% conversion
+- ops/dev are set via set_daily_spend; targeted ops can override
+- Context is erased weekly — MUST save reasoning in files
 
-## Metrics to Track This Week
-- New subscriber count (baseline: 0, target: ANY positive number)
-- Open issues count (current: 182, target: declining)
-- Cancellations (current: 9/week, target: <10)
-- Cash trajectory
-- Lead conversion rate (target: >5%)
-- Enterprise turns (target: any)
+## Strategy Going Forward
+- Primary: Extend runway as long as possible
+- If market shifts or we can afford R&D tier 3+ ($500K+), invest
+- If churn stays manageable with T2, keep; if it spikes, reassess
+- No point spending on acquisition until conversion returns
